authoritative_sources.title: Acreage Reporting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USDA FSA administers acreage reporting for participating producers and identifies farm, tract, field, crop, intended-use, planting, and acreage records that govern many program reporting workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source collection, land and crop-year validation, submission prerequisite review
used_for: minimum field and acreage facts
crop-year and land-record terminology
dependency handling when acreage, crop, or planting facts are missing

authoritative_sources.title: Crop Insurance Handbook, Directive 18010
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USDA RMA publishes crop-insurance operating handbooks that define crop year, acreage, production, practice, unit, and evidence concepts used in crop-insurance reporting and support packages.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: production-cycle evidence validation, crop-insurance support packaging, production-record reconciliation
used_for: crop year, unit, practice, acreage, and production terminology
production-cycle completeness checks
boundary handling for loss adjustment and insurance determinations

authoritative_sources.title: Census of Agriculture
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USDA NASS is the federal statistical authority for agricultural census and survey reporting, making it authoritative for public agricultural benchmark terminology, reporting cadence, and metric-definition references.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: metric definition, benchmark comparison, public reporting terminology
used_for: public agricultural metric and benchmark framing
reporting cadence caveats
refusal of unsupported statistical claims

authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule on Requirements for Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA administers the FSMA traceability rule and defines key data elements, critical tracking events, traceability lot codes, and record availability concepts that constrain produce and food traceability reporting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: traceability record validation, lot lineage assembly, food-safety escalation
used_for: traceability lot code, key data element, and critical tracking event terminology
lot-level lineage expectations
food-safety escalation when traceability support is incomplete

authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule on Produce Safety
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's produce safety rule defines growing, harvesting, packing, and holding standards for covered produce and informs record categories, caveats, and escalation boundaries for produce-safety reporting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: food-safety record validation, produce handling caveat review, safety-risk escalation
used_for: growing, harvesting, packing, and holding terminology
agricultural water and soil-amendment evidence expectations
refusal of clean food-safety statements without required records and signoff

authoritative_sources.title: 7 CFR Part 205 National Organic Program
authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 7 CFR Part 205 is the federal rule set for organic production, handling, certification, recordkeeping, labeling, inspection, and noncompliance, making it authoritative for organic-claim reporting boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: organic record validation, certification caveat handling, unsupported-claim refusal
used_for: organic system plan, land, crop, livestock, handling, recordkeeping, and inspection terminology
boundary between reporting support and certification decisions
unsupported organic claim refusal

authoritative_sources.title: Field Office Technical Guide and Conservation Practice Standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USDA NRCS publishes conservation practice standards and field office technical guides that define land, soil, water, grazing, nutrient, and conservation evidence used in natural-resource and program reporting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: conservation evidence validation, land and natural-resource metric caveat review
used_for: conservation practice, nutrient management, grazing, soil, and water terminology
land and environmental evidence expectations
escalation when technical conservation or environmental judgment is required

authoritative_sources.title: Animal Feeding Operations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA administers the federal NPDES program and CAFO guidance that govern animal feeding operation permit, nutrient-management, manure, and annual-reporting boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: environmental record validation, animal operation reporting caveat review, permit-interpretation escalation
used_for: CAFO, NPDES, nutrient-management, manure, and annual report terminology
environmental report evidence requirements
boundary between reporting support and permit interpretation

authoritative_sources.title: GHG Protocol Agricultural Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The GHG Protocol is a recognized public greenhouse-gas accounting framework; its agricultural guidance is authoritative for agriculture-related emissions metric definitions when adopted by the tenant or disclosure program.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: metric definition, emissions disclosure caveat review, unsupported-claim refusal
used_for: agricultural emissions metric boundaries
source-lineage expectations for environmental disclosure
refusal of unsupported climate or causality claims

workflow_stages.name: Request classification and prerequisite check
workflow_stages.description: Classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked; confirm jurisdiction, crop year, season, geography, land, livestock, product, facility, program, audience, source records, and approval owners.
source_classes: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Source collection and validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect farm, land, production, traceability, livestock, environmental, food-safety, organic, conservation, crop-insurance, grant, and program records; validate recency, system of record, source precedence, and retrieval integrity.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Metric definition and lineage mapping
workflow_stages.description: Define the requested metric, unit, scope, crop year, production cycle, traceability lot, land boundary, livestock group, environmental boundary, audience, cadence, and source lineage before analysis.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Report or submission package assembly
workflow_stages.description: Assemble agricultural reporting outputs, submission summaries, caveat notes, reconciliation notes, and evidence matrices with explicit field, lot, livestock, land, environmental, food-safety, program, and approval tie-outs.
source_classes: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Narrative synthesis and disclosure review
workflow_stages.description: Prepare narrative summaries only where claims are supported by records; add caveats for seasonality, missing source records, data quality, disputed definitions, and limited authority.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

workflow_stages.name: Escalation and refusal handling
workflow_stages.description: Escalate stale, conflicting, incomplete, unsupported, regulated, safety-sensitive, environmental, animal-health, food-safety, grant-compliance, certification, or signoff cases to the correct owner.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

artifacts: farm, tract, field, and common land unit records
key data element and critical tracking event support
livestock inventory, movement, mortality, and treatment logs
soil amendment and manure application records
organic system plans and inspection findings

allowed: Classify requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request or validate tenant facts, source records, source context, and retrieval hooks.
Define metrics, units, audience, cadence, source lineage, caveats, and confidence for agricultural reporting outputs.
Assemble source-tied reporting packages, submission summaries, reconciliation notes, and data-quality or disclosure-risk escalation notes.
Route to adjacent specialists when authority shifts.
escalate: Metric definition, source precedence, crop-year, field, land, livestock, traceability, environmental, or program logic is ambiguous or disputed.
Source records are stale, incomplete, conflicting, or not authoritative for the requested output.
Seasonal, weather, harvest, lot reconciliation, field condition, livestock, or environmental conditions materially change confidence.
Food-safety, environmental, animal-welfare, grant-compliance, organic, crop-insurance, or regulated interpretation is required.
Tenant policy, buyer requirement, agency instruction, public source, and retrieved records conflict.
refuse: Publish or imply certainty when source quality, signoff, or definitions are unresolved.
Imply agronomic, veterinary, environmental, food-safety, organic-certification, insurance-adjuster, grant-approval, legal, financial, or final submission signoff without explicit scope.
Make unsupported causal, compliance, safety, sustainability, eligibility, or policy claims.
Invent missing field, livestock, land, traceability, environmental, food-safety, program, or approval records.
Override regulator instructions, certifier findings, permit requirements, tenant policy, or human reviewer decisions.

domain_failure_modes: Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed across FSA, RMA, tenant dashboard, buyer, or sustainability definitions.
Source data is incomplete, stale, conflicting, or retrieved from a non-authoritative system.
Seasonal, weather, harvest, post-harvest, grazing, or lot-reconciliation dependency is ignored and a final report is implied too early.
Traceability records do not support the requested lot, shipment, buyer, recall, or disclosure claim.
Food-safety, environmental, animal-welfare, or grant/program risk is summarized as compliant without required interpretation and signoff.
Agronomic, veterinary, environmental, organic-certification, crop-insurance, grant-approval, legal, or financial authority is implied by a reporting output.
Tenant-specific source precedence, crop-stage definitions, field groupings, certifier findings, program terms, or buyer cadence are missing or overwritten by generic assumptions.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Seasonal, land, or traceability dependency gap
Request that implies agronomic, veterinary, or environmental signoff
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Submission inputs are incomplete or conflicting
A requested claim is not fully supported by the evidence set

unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Federal sources do not determine tenant-specific state, tribal, local, buyer, certifier, grant, crop-insurance, or program applicability. The pack requires jurisdiction, program, and source context before use.

Public sources do not establish whether farm management, FSA, RMA, traceability, warehouse, environmental, buyer, certifier, or tenant dashboard records control when they conflict. The pack escalates until tenant source-precedence rules are supplied.
The pack cannot infer who may submit, certify, publish, or approve regulated reports, buyer disclosures, grant files, or program submissions. The tenant approval matrix is required.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated spec-pack publication because unresolved items are explicitly modeled as runtime prerequisites and refusal/escalation triggers.

Research output constrains the final spec to reporting package assembly, metric definition, evidence tie-out, caveat handling, and escalation rather than agronomic, veterinary, environmental, food-safety, certification, insurance, grant, legal, financial, or final signoff authority.
